metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either scope the SP 800-53 / SSDF benchmark comparison to a specific, bounded set of control families (e.g., IA, AU, SC) that are directly addressed by the described controls, or create a stub mapping artifact the ADR can reference — do not rely on ungrounded from-scratch authorship at ADR-write time.
findings.finding: AC-4 requires the ADR to include a prior-art/benchmark section covering NIST SP 800-218 (SSDF) and SP 800-53, but no existing SP 800-53 or SSDF gap analysis or control mapping exists anywhere in the corpus (ADR 0044, ADR 0045, remediation-implementation-plan, oss-comparison, and all idea tickets are silent on these frameworks). The ADR's author must produce this mapping from scratch with no grounding material, yet the plan provides no guidance on scope, depth, or which specific SP 800-53 control families or SSDF practices are claimed covered/not-covered.
findings.location: AC-4: 'The ADR includes a prior-art/benchmark section comparing against … NIST SP 800-218 (SSDF) / SP 800-53'

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the 5-limitations-to-3-tickets mismatch by adding an explicit mapping (e.g., a table or inline notation in the Context or AC5) showing which ticket covers each of L1–L5.
findings.finding: The Context states 'each [of the five limitations] with its … tracked deferred-hardening idea ticket' (implying a 1-to-1 mapping), but AC5 names only three ticket IDs (2422, 4e1d, 5100) for five limitations (L1-L5). The mapping between the five limitations and the three tickets is unspecified, leaving an implementer uncertain which ticket(s) cover which limitation(s) — or whether some limitations intentionally share a ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short mapping in AC5 or the Context, e.g.: 'L1→2422, L2→2422, L3→4e1d, L4→5100, L5→5100' (or whichever grouping is intended), so reviewers can verify coverage deterministically.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify CVE-2026-44544 exists as a public record for gittuf (or replace with the actual gittuf issue/PR/commit that motivated the monotonic policy counter); if no public record exists, reframe the ticket-2422 rationale using the gittuf design doc or a specific commit reference instead.
findings.finding: Ticket 2422 — which the ADR will reference as a deferred-hardening cross-link — cites 'CVE-2026-44544' as a real gittuf vulnerability ('gittuf shipped the same log-position model and still needed a monotonic policy counter (CVE-2026-44544)'). This CVE identifier is future-dated and unverifiable against any public CVE database or gittuf issue tracker; it appears to be a fabricated illustrative identifier rather than a confirmed public vulnerability record. If the ADR reproduces or inherits this claim, it embeds an unverified (and potentially hallucinated) factual assertion in a security-assurance document.
findings.location: Ticket 2422 description (deferred-hardening reference for L1); plan AC line: 'Deferred-hardening items reference the tracked idea tickets 2422, 4e1d, and 5100'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the CVE citation in ticket 2422 with a link to the actual gittuf policy-versioning design rationale (e.g., the gittuf spec section on policy versioning, or a specific GitHub issue/PR if one exists). If a real CVE is intended, confirm it is published and link to nvd.nist.gov before the ADR lands.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether L3 (TOFU-authorship) and L5 (process-not-correctness) each have a deferred-hardening idea ticket; if so, add those ticket IDs to AC5; if these limitations are fully accepted with no planned future hardening, explicitly state that in the plan so the ADR can document them accordingly.
findings.finding: The plan names only three deferred-hardening tickets (2422, 4e1d, 5100) for five accepted limitations (L1–L5), but AC3 implies each limitation carries its own tracked deferred-hardening idea ticket. L3 (TOFU-authorship) and L5 (process-not-correctness) have no named ticket, so the ADR cannot satisfy AC3's per-limitation ticket requirement as written.
findings.location: AC3 ('each of the five findings (L1-L5) is framed as an accepted limitation carrying a bounding control and an authority tier') and AC5 ('Deferred-hardening items reference the tracked idea tickets 2422, 4e1d, and 5100')
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC5 to either (a) list all five ticket IDs (one per limitation), or (b) explicitly note that L3 and L5 are 'no deferred hardening planned — fully accepted' so the ADR can say 'no tracked follow-up' for those two rather than carrying an implicit gap.

findings.finding: AC4 ('The ADR lands on main via Gerrit (Verified +1, LLM-Review +1)') requires external Gerrit vote outcomes, which live outside the codebase and cannot be produced in-session by the executing agent. Per ADR 0043, such criteria must carry the `[operator-attested]` prefix so reviewers know the proving evidence is a recorded attestation (change ID / vote outcome) rather than an in-session command. Without the tag, this AC appears to demand an in-session proving command that does not exist.
findings.suggested_fix: - [x] [operator-attested] The ADR lands on main via Gerrit (Verified +1, LLM-Review +1).

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
